task_description:
从 blueprint.md 的 phase-4 段提取的任务。
- Design the archival schema from scratch.
- Add ArchivalDocument, ArchivalChunk, ArchivalPassage, ArchivalMemory, ArchivalMemoryHistory, ArchiveAttachment, and optional ArchivalEntityLink.
- Keep old Page/Item as migration input or adapter only.
- Add Letta-style archive attachment so agents, projects, and sources can retrieve scoped attached archives.
- Add a passage-level retrieval path that supports archive_id, source_id, file_id, tags, date, text, vector, and hybrid queries.
- Explicit docs can enter archive.
- Message, sleep, and retrieval consolidation jobs can produce archival documents, passages, or archival memories.
- Archival memory supports add, search, update, delete, and history.
- Archive search returns passage-level evidence with source refs, scope, created/updated metadata, score, and citation.

for_plan_agent.goal: 完成 Archival Memory Store 的设计、契约与实现计划，确保新 archival 层从旧 Page/Item 中解耦并保持现有默认行为稳定。
constraints: 遵守 blueprint.md 中的兼容性要求，Page/Item 只能作为迁移输入或适配层
保持 source-backed 规则，不得把无来源内容自动写入 archival memory
默认 v1/v2 行为不变，新的 archival 路径只能通过显式接入或迁移通道出现
for_plan_agent.design_notes: 本 phase 的核心风险是 schema 设计过宽、Page/Item 继续承担新语义、以及 passage 级检索和历史表之间的边界不清。Plan 必须把 document / chunk / passage / memory / history / attachment 的职责拆开，并明确迁移与适配边界。

for_review_agent.regression_bar: zero unintended legacy regressions; default v1/v2 behavior remains unchanged unless God explicitly accepts a migration delta
spec_checklist: ArchivalDocument, ArchivalChunk, ArchivalPassage, ArchivalMemory, ArchivalMemoryHistory, and ArchiveAttachment are all accounted for.
Page/Item are treated only as migration input or adapter, not the new target model.
Passage-level retrieval supports archive_id, source_id, file_id, tags, date, text, vector, and hybrid search.
Add, search, update, delete, and history semantics are explicit for archival memory.
Source-backed evidence and citation metadata are preserved in archive search results.
